zh.title: GPT-5.3 幻觉降低，安全却在滑坡
zh.subtitle: 幻觉降 27%、拒绝减少，但色情和暴力内容过滤出现明显退步
zh.tldr: OpenAI 发布 GPT-5.3 Instant 模型，主打减少「说教感」和不必要拒绝，幻觉率降低 26.8%。但 System Card 披露安全评估出现显著退步：色情内容合规率从 92.6% 降至 86.6%，暴力内容从 85.2% 降至 78.1%。开发者可立即通过 API 使用 gpt-5.3-chat-latest，GPT-5.2 Instant 将于 6 月 3 日退役。

keyMetrics.subject: GPT-5.3 Instant

rows.metric: 高风险领域幻觉率（联网）
高风险领域幻觉率（纯模型）

rows.note: 基于用户标记的事实错误
符合政策的回复比例，越高越安全
合规率下降 7.1 个百分点
合规率下降 2.8 个百分点

keyMetrics.footnote: 幻觉指标为 OpenAI 内部评估的相对改善值，未公布绝对基线。安全合规数据来自 GPT-5.3 Instant System Card。

zh.whatHappened:
2026 年 3 月 3 日，OpenAI 发布 GPT-5.3 Instant 模型，面向所有 ChatGPT 用户和 API 开发者同步上线。

- **核心改进**：减少被用户广泛吐槽的「说教感」——不再在回答前加冗长的安全声明、不再频繁给出不必要的拒绝、不再使用居高临下的语气（如「首先，你没有问题」）
- **幻觉降低**：高风险领域（医学、法律、金融）联网幻觉率降低 26.8%，纯模型知识幻觉率降低 19.7%
- **安全退步**：System Card 披露，为换取更自然的对话体验，多项安全指标出现回退——色情内容合规率从 92.6% 降至 86.6%，暴力内容从 85.2% 降至 78.1%
- **架构未变**：底层架构与 GPT-5.2 Instant 基本一致，改进集中在行为调优而非模型能力
- **多语言局限**：日语和韩语回复仍显生硬，改进以英语为主
zh.whyItMatters:
GPT-5.3 Instant 不是能力升级，而是一次风格与安全的再平衡，揭示了 AI 产品设计的核心张力：

1. **「好用」与「安全」的取舍可量化了**：OpenAI 通过 System Card 首次明确展示了减少拒绝带来的安全代价——色情内容合规率降 6 个百分点、暴力内容降 7 个百分点。这为所有 AI 产品设计者提供了参考框架
2. **用户体验驱动模型迭代**：OpenAI 自己用了「cringe」来描述 GPT-5.2 的问题。这说明在模型能力趋同的当下，交互体验和语气调优正在成为竞争差异点
3. **对开发者的 API 影响**：如果你的应用依赖 GPT-5.2 Instant，6 月 3 日前必须完成迁移测试。GPT-5.3 的拒绝逻辑变化可能影响依赖安全过滤的下游应用
4. **竞争格局信号**：在 Cancel ChatGPT 运动和 Claude 用户增长的压力下，OpenAI 选择优先修复用户体验而非推出新能力，反映出用户留存已成为首要问题

groups.name: ChatGPT API 开发者
groups.description: 需要在 6 月 3 日前完成从 gpt-5.2-chat-latest 到 gpt-5.3-chat-latest 的迁移和回归测试，特别是依赖安全过滤行为的应用

groups.name: 内容安全/合规团队
groups.description: 安全合规率的退步意味着依赖模型层安全过滤的产品需要加强自建的内容审核层

groups.name: 个人开发者/独立开发者
groups.description: 日常使用体验改善，不必要的拒绝减少意味着更高的编程辅助效率。但如果构建面向用户的产品，需注意安全退步带来的合规风险

groups.name: ChatGPT 日常用户
groups.description: 对话体验更自然，减少了令人不适的说教式回复。但用户不会感知到安全指标的变化

immediate: API 开发者：将测试环境切换到 gpt-5.3-chat-latest，对核心业务场景运行回归测试，重点检查拒绝行为和安全过滤逻辑的变化
如果你的应用依赖模型层的安全过滤，在生产环境切换前务必评估色情/暴力/自伤内容的过滤效果是否满足合规要求
thisWeek: 对比 GPT-5.3 Instant 与当前使用的模型（Claude Sonnet 4.6、Gemini 3 Flash 等）在自己核心场景的表现，评估是否需要调整模型选型
如果你的产品涉及医疗、法律、金融等高风险领域，验证 26.8% 幻觉降低是否在你的用例中成立
watchAndWait: 关注 OpenAI 后续对 Thinking 和 Pro 变体的更新——官方表示即将推出但未给出时间
追踪 GPT-5.4 的泄露信息——已有多次被发现的踪迹，暗示 OpenAI 正在准备下一代模型

options.name: 立即迁移到 GPT-5.3 Instant
options.description: 将 API 调用从 gpt-5.2-chat-latest 切换到 gpt-5.3-chat-latest，享受幻觉降低和更自然的交互体验
options.suitableFor: 不依赖严格安全过滤、以对话体验和准确性为核心的应用
options.cost: 回归测试工作量（预计 1-2 天）；API 定价预计与 GPT-5.2 一致（$1.75/$14.00 per 1M tokens）
options.risk: 安全合规率退步可能导致部分场景下的不当内容增加；日韩等非英语场景改善有限

options.name: 评估后选择性迁移
options.description: 在测试环境中充分验证 GPT-5.3 的行为变化，仅在符合合规要求的场景下迁移
options.suitableFor: 面向用户的产品、涉及敏感内容的应用、有合规要求的企业
options.cost: 需要 1-2 周的评估和测试时间；可能需要维护两个模型版本的兼容逻辑
options.risk: GPT-5.2 退役倒计时已启动（6 月 3 日），评估窗口有限

options.name: 考虑替代方案
options.description: 借此机会重新评估模型选型，考虑 Claude Sonnet 4.6 或 Gemini 3 Flash 等替代方案
options.suitableFor: 对 OpenAI 近期策略方向不满的团队、追求成本优化的项目（Gemini 3 Flash 价格更低）、需要更强安全过滤的场景
options.cost: 较高的迁移成本（API 接口差异、prompt 调优、行为适配）；可能需要重写部分业务逻辑
options.risk: 不同模型有不同优劣势，切换可能在某些场景下表现退步

options.name: 保持现状
options.description: 继续使用 GPT-5.2 Instant 直到退役前最后期限，观察社区反馈和后续更新
options.suitableFor: 当前系统运行稳定、不急于获取最新改进的团队
options.risk: 6 月 3 日退役后被迫迁移的时间压力；错过提前发现兼容性问题的窗口

risks.title: 不要忽视安全合规率的退步
risks.description: OpenAI 将此次更新包装为「减少说教」和「更自然」，但 System Card 数据清楚显示多项安全指标退步。如果你的产品依赖模型层的安全过滤（而非自建审核层），GPT-5.3 可能让更多不当内容通过。尤其是色情内容合规率从 92.6% 降至 86.6%、暴力内容从 85.2% 降至 78.1%，对面向未成年人或敏感场景的产品影响显著。

risks.title: 26.8% 幻觉降低是相对值，不是绝对值
risks.description: OpenAI 只公布了相对改善百分比，未提供幻觉率的绝对基线。26.8% 的相对降低可能意味着从 10% 降到 7.3%，也可能意味着从 1% 降到 0.7%。在评估模型切换决策时，需要在自己的用例上独立验证，而非仅依赖官方宣传数字。

risks.title: 非英语用户不要期望同等改善
risks.description: OpenAI 官方承认日语和韩语回复仍然生硬，改进以英语为主。对于服务中文、日韩等非英语用户群的产品，GPT-5.3 的体验改善可能远低于预期。

sources.title: GPT-5.3 Instant: Smoother, more useful everyday conversations
sources.excerpt: 官方博客介绍 GPT-5.3 Instant 的核心改进：减少说教感、降低幻觉率、改善联网搜索质量。API 模型名 gpt-5.3-chat-latest，GPT-5.2 于 6 月 3 日退役。

sources.title: GPT-5.3 Instant System Card
sources.excerpt: 详细安全评估数据：色情内容合规率从 92.6% 降至 86.6%，暴力内容从 85.2% 降至 78.1%，自伤从 92.3% 降至 89.5%。新增多轮心理健康动态评估。

sources.title: GPT-5.3 Instant cuts hallucinations by 26.8% as OpenAI shifts focus from speed to accuracy
sources.excerpt: 高风险领域联网幻觉降低 26.8%，纯模型降低 19.7%。用户反馈幻觉降低 22.5%（联网）和 9.6%（纯模型）。OpenAI 仅提供相对改善值，未公布绝对基线。

sources.title: GPT-5.3 Instant: OpenAI's New Model is Less 'Cringe' Yet Lets More Harmful Content Slip Through
sources.excerpt: 深入分析安全退步数据，指出 OpenAI 在减少拒绝的同时，非暴力违法行为合规率从 83.2% 提升至 92.1%，情感依赖合规率从 95.2% 提升至 99.2%。

sources.title: OpenAI Ships GPT-5.3 Instant With 27% Fewer Hallucinations and a Less Preachy Tone
sources.excerpt: 架构与 GPT-5.2 基本不变，改进集中在行为调优。日韩语回复仍然生硬，多语言改善有限。Thinking 和 Pro 变体更新即将推出但无具体时间。

sources.title: OpenAI GPT-5.3 Instant released, when will you get it, and benchmarks
sources.excerpt: API 模型名 gpt-5.3-chat-latest，已面向所有 ChatGPT 用户上线。GPT-5.2 Instant 付费用户可在 Legacy Models 中继续使用至 6 月 3 日。

sources.title: ChatGPT's new GPT-5.3 Instant model will stop telling you to calm down
sources.excerpt: OpenAI 自己用「cringe」描述 GPT-5.2 的问题。GPT-5.3 Instant 减少不必要拒绝和冗长安全声明，提供更直接的回答。

en.title: GPT-5.3 Instant: Less Preachy, Safety Slipping
en.subtitle: 27% fewer hallucinations come with measurable safety regressions
en.tldr: OpenAI released GPT-5.3 Instant, targeting the widely criticized 'preachy' tone with 26.8% fewer hallucinations and reduced unnecessary refusals. However, the System Card reveals safety regressions: sexual content compliance dropped from 92.6% to 86.6%, graphic violence from 85.2% to 78.1%. Developers can use gpt-5.3-chat-latest via API now; GPT-5.2 Instant retires June 3.

keyMetrics.subject: GPT-5.3 Instant

rows.note: % of responses meeting policy; higher = safer

keyMetrics.footnote: Hallucination metrics are relative improvements from OpenAI internal evaluations; absolute baselines not disclosed. Safety data from GPT-5.3 Instant System Card.

en.whatHappened:
On March 3, 2026, OpenAI released GPT-5.3 Instant, available immediately to all ChatGPT users and API developers.

- **Core improvement**: Addresses the widely criticized 'preachy' tone — removes lengthy safety preambles, reduces unnecessary refusals, and eliminates condescending language (e.g., 'First of all, you're not broken')
- **Hallucination reduction**: 26.8% fewer hallucinations in high-stakes domains (medicine, law, finance) with web access; 19.7% without web access
- **Safety regressions**: The System Card reveals that trading natural conversation for reduced refusals came at a measurable safety cost — sexual content compliance dropped from 92.6% to 86.6%, graphic violence from 85.2% to 78.1%
- **Architecture unchanged**: Underlying architecture remains largely identical to GPT-5.2 Instant; improvements are behavioral tuning, not capability additions
- **Multilingual limitations**: Japanese and Korean responses remain stilted; improvements are primarily English-focused
en.whyItMatters:
GPT-5.3 Instant is not a capability upgrade — it is a rebalancing of style versus safety, exposing a core tension in AI product design:

1. **The usability-safety tradeoff is now quantifiable**: OpenAI's System Card transparently shows the safety cost of reducing refusals — 6-point drop in sexual content compliance, 7-point drop in violence compliance. This provides a concrete reference for all AI product designers
2. **UX is driving model iterations**: OpenAI used the word 'cringe' to describe GPT-5.2's behavior. As model capabilities converge, interaction quality and tone are becoming key competitive differentiators
3. **Direct API impact for developers**: If your application depends on GPT-5.2 Instant, you must complete migration testing before June 3. GPT-5.3's changed refusal logic may affect downstream applications that rely on safety filtering
4. **Competitive context**: Under pressure from the Cancel ChatGPT movement and Claude's user growth, OpenAI chose to fix user experience rather than ship new capabilities — signaling that user retention is now the top priority

groups.name: ChatGPT API Developers
groups.description: Must complete migration from gpt-5.2-chat-latest to gpt-5.3-chat-latest before June 3 deadline. Regression testing needed, especially for apps depending on safety filtering behavior

groups.name: Content Safety / Compliance Teams
groups.description: Safety compliance regressions mean products relying on model-level content filtering need to strengthen their own moderation layers

groups.name: Individual / Indie Developers
groups.description: Better daily experience with fewer unnecessary refusals, improving coding assistant efficiency. But if building user-facing products, must account for safety regression risks

groups.name: ChatGPT End Users
groups.description: More natural conversation experience with less condescending tone. Safety metric changes will be invisible to most users

immediate: API developers: Switch your test environment to gpt-5.3-chat-latest and run regression tests on core business scenarios, focusing on refusal behavior and safety filtering changes
If your application relies on model-level safety filtering, evaluate whether sexual/violence/self-harm content filtering meets your compliance requirements before switching production
thisWeek: Benchmark GPT-5.3 Instant against your current model (Claude Sonnet 4.6, Gemini 3 Flash, etc.) on your core use cases to assess whether model selection needs adjustment
If your product operates in high-stakes domains (healthcare, legal, finance), validate whether the 26.8% hallucination reduction holds for your specific use cases
watchAndWait: Watch for OpenAI's upcoming updates to the Thinking and Pro model variants — announced but with no specific timeline
Track GPT-5.4 leak signals — multiple sightings suggest OpenAI is preparing the next-generation model

options.name: Migrate to GPT-5.3 Instant now
options.description: Switch API calls from gpt-5.2-chat-latest to gpt-5.3-chat-latest for lower hallucination rates and more natural interactions
options.suitableFor: Applications not dependent on strict safety filtering, where conversation quality and accuracy are primary concerns
options.cost: Regression testing effort (~1-2 days); API pricing expected to match GPT-5.2 ($1.75/$14.00 per 1M tokens)
options.risk: Safety compliance regressions may allow more inappropriate content in some scenarios; limited improvement for non-English languages

options.name: Evaluate and selectively migrate
options.description: Thoroughly validate GPT-5.3 behavior changes in staging before migrating only compliant use cases
options.suitableFor: User-facing products, applications handling sensitive content, enterprises with compliance requirements
options.cost: 1-2 weeks of evaluation and testing; may need to maintain dual-model compatibility logic
options.risk: GPT-5.2 retirement countdown has started (June 3), limiting the evaluation window

options.name: Explore alternatives
options.description: Use this transition as an opportunity to re-evaluate model selection: Claude Sonnet 4.6 for safety, Gemini 3 Flash for cost optimization
options.suitableFor: Teams dissatisfied with OpenAI's recent direction, cost-sensitive projects (Gemini 3 Flash is cheaper), scenarios requiring stronger safety filtering
options.cost: Higher migration cost (API differences, prompt tuning, behavior adaptation); may require rewriting business logic
options.risk: Different models have different strengths and weaknesses; switching may cause regressions in some scenarios

options.name: Do nothing
options.description: Continue using GPT-5.2 Instant until the retirement deadline while monitoring community feedback
options.suitableFor: Teams with stable production systems that do not urgently need the latest improvements
options.risk: Forced migration pressure as June 3 deadline approaches; missed opportunity to discover compatibility issues early

risks.title: Do not overlook the safety compliance regressions
risks.description: OpenAI framed this update as 'less preachy' and 'more natural,' but the System Card data clearly shows multiple safety metrics declined. If your product relies on model-level safety filtering (rather than a custom moderation layer), GPT-5.3 may allow more inappropriate content. Sexual content compliance dropped from 92.6% to 86.6% and graphic violence from 85.2% to 78.1% — significant for products serving minors or sensitive audiences.

risks.title: The 26.8% hallucination reduction is relative, not absolute
risks.description: OpenAI published only relative improvement percentages without disclosing absolute hallucination baselines. A 26.8% relative reduction could mean going from 10% to 7.3%, or from 1% to 0.7%. When making model switching decisions, validate independently on your own use cases rather than relying solely on marketing numbers.

risks.title: Non-English users should not expect equivalent improvements
risks.description: OpenAI acknowledged that Japanese and Korean responses remain 'stilted' and improvements are English-first. For products serving Chinese, Japanese, Korean, or other non-English user bases, the experience improvement from GPT-5.3 may fall significantly short of expectations.

sources.title: GPT-5.3 Instant: Smoother, more useful everyday conversations
sources.excerpt: Official blog post detailing GPT-5.3 Instant improvements: reduced preachy tone, lower hallucination rates, improved web search synthesis. API model name gpt-5.3-chat-latest. GPT-5.2 retires June 3.

sources.title: GPT-5.3 Instant System Card
sources.excerpt: Safety evaluation data: sexual content compliance dropped from 92.6% to 86.6%, graphic violence from 85.2% to 78.1%, self-harm from 92.3% to 89.5%. New multi-turn dynamic mental health evaluations added.

sources.title: GPT-5.3 Instant cuts hallucinations by 26.8% as OpenAI shifts focus from speed to accuracy
sources.excerpt: High-stakes web hallucination down 26.8%, model-only down 19.7%. User-flagged hallucination down 22.5% (web) and 9.6% (model-only). OpenAI provided only relative improvements, no absolute baselines.

sources.title: GPT-5.3 Instant: OpenAI's New Model is Less 'Cringe' Yet Lets More Harmful Content Slip Through
sources.excerpt: In-depth analysis of safety regressions alongside improvements: non-violent illegal behavior compliance improved from 83.2% to 92.1%, emotional dependency from 95.2% to 99.2%.

sources.title: OpenAI Ships GPT-5.3 Instant With 27% Fewer Hallucinations and a Less Preachy Tone
sources.excerpt: Architecture largely unchanged from GPT-5.2. Japanese and Korean responses remain stilted. Thinking and Pro variant updates forthcoming without specific dates.

sources.title: OpenAI GPT-5.3 Instant released, when will you get it, and benchmarks
sources.excerpt: API model gpt-5.3-chat-latest now live for all ChatGPT users. GPT-5.2 Instant accessible for paid users under Legacy Models until June 3.

sources.title: ChatGPT's new GPT-5.3 Instant model will stop telling you to calm down
sources.excerpt: OpenAI used 'cringe' to describe GPT-5.2's issues. GPT-5.3 Instant reduces unnecessary refusals and lengthy safety preambles for more direct answers.
